name: python3-plaso
title: python3-plaso
summary: super timeline all the things -- Python 3
description:
super timeline all the things -- Python 3
Plaso (plaso langar að safna öllu) is the Python based back-end
engine used by tools such as log2timeline for automatic creation of a
super timelines. The goal of log2timeline (and thus plaso) is to
provide a single tool that can parse various log files and forensic
artifacts from computers and related systems, such as network
equipment to produce a single correlated timeline. This timeline can
then be easily analysed by forensic investigators/analysts, speeding
up investigations by correlating the vast amount of information found
on an average computer system.
.
This package contains a Plaso installation for Python 3.

arguments.description: Option present in package usage line for plaso-image_export.
arguments.raw: -image_export  Option present in package usage line for plaso-image_export.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use plaso-image_export because super timeline all the things -- Python 3.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use plaso-image_export as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `plaso-image_export [options] [args]` as documented upstream for plaso-image_export.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair plaso-image_export with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `plaso-image_export --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.name: -image_export
arguments.description: Option present in package usage line for plaso-image_export.
